step.title: Generate i18n translator-foundation goal (single goal, phase=cross-cutting)
step.rule: Scaffold goal (phase=scaffold) MUST exist in goals.yaml before this step runs — the translator foundation depends only on the repo skeleton.

This step is UNCONDITIONAL — the php-symfony pack carries PHP-I18N-001 as a `must` code-rule, so every greenfield plan establishes a single-locale translator foundation even while ADR-013 defers full i18n. Never split.

Single-locale only (English per ADR-013): this goal establishes the translator config + a TranslatableDomainException base so user-facing strings/backend errors are translatable. It does NOT add a second locale, locale negotiation, or per-string extraction — that is the implementer's downstream concern under PHP-I18N-001.
depends_on = [SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID] ONLY — never the domain/error-handling goals (no rewiring; scope guard).

substep.title: Collect scaffold goal ID
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Filter for phase=scaffold. Store the goal ID as SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID (goal-002 in a fresh plan).

check.action: ABORT with error: "No scaffold goal (phase=scaffold) found in goals.yaml — cannot generate translator-foundation goal without the repo skeleton"

substep.rule: SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID MUST be dynamically resolved from goals.yaml — never hardcoded (G-03).

substep.title: Derive the coarse deliverable_area
substep.action: Set I18N_FOUNDATION_AREA = the translator-foundation deliverable directory in the resolved topology — contexts/shared/src/Exception/ (the contexts/shared shared-kernel exception directory the shard's old DELIVERABLES referenced, home of the TranslatableDomainException base every other context depends on), where contexts/shared is the published shared-kernel context from the resolved conventions/topology. This is a COARSE path (the depinfer produce/consume seed + the elaborator's tree-read anchor), NOT a glob and NOT a per-file path.
substep.rule: Do NOT read layout.md to bind SRC_ROOTS, resolve translation.yaml, or author any acceptance/validate/investigator/not-in-scope/scope here — those are Tier-2 (/tmux:elaborate authors them at dispatch, including the genuine PHP-I18N-001 cross-cutting injection). The roadmap skeleton commits only to the coarse deliverable_area.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP and record ID (roadmap skeleton)
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with a roadmap SKELETON:

params.param: Establish single-locale translator foundation + TranslatableDomainException base (PHP-I18N-001)
[SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID] — the scaffold goal ID collected in substep 3.21b.1 (NEVER the domain/error-handling goals)

substep.rule: Description is 94 characters — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=3 (G-11) — a focused single-locale foundation, fixable in few cycles.

phase=cross-cutting on the created goal — a REAL Go rule phase, so PHP-I18N-001 genuinely injects at dispatch (the whole point of this goal). It shares PHASE_ORDER rank=10 with the other cross-cutting phases (added in substep 4.2), so it orders legally.

NO validate/acceptance/scope/context/investigation_config/not_in_scope params — this is a roadmap skeleton; /tmux:elaborate authors every concrete field at dispatch (design §5/§6). NEVER run `tmux-cli rules match` here.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Record the returned goal ID as I18N_FOUNDATION_GOAL_ID.
Print: "Translator foundation skeleton created: goal-{{ID}} (phase=cross-cutting, status=roadmap, depends_on=[{{SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID}}], deliverable_area={{I18N_FOUNDATION_AREA}})"
